title: Build a useLocalStorage Hook
prompt: Build a `useLocalStorage<T>(key: string, initialValue: T)` hook that works like `useState` but persists values to `localStorage`. On mount it should read the stored value (falling back to `initialValue`). On every update it should write to `localStorage`.
context: `useLocalStorage` is a classic hook composition problem. The tricky part is initialization — `localStorage` is not available during SSR, and JSON parse can fail on corrupt stored data. The setter should accept both a value and an updater function (matching the `useState` API).
